ab: ai makaleketa ko kisalro mosowa sa ‘aiso?
en: A: Can I borrow money from you?

ab: eene, ai kisalro so sa thaino?
en: B: Yes! How much do you need?

ab: marimora ko ‘iloko sa ‘aiso, ai maka kisalro ko mosowa sa ’aiso?
ch: A:我忘記帶錢了，我可以跟你借錢嗎?
en: A: I forgot to bring money, can I borrow money from you?

ab: miya lriyali kadro iloko sa ‘aiso!
en: B: I don't have any money either!

ab: marimora ko ‘iloko sa ‘aiso, ai maka kisalrowa ko mosowa sa ’aiso?
ch: A:我忘記帶錢了，我可以跟你借錢嗎?
en: A: I forgot to bring money, can I borrow money from you?

ab: ini, kai kisalro so nakowa ka ‘aiso ka ‘aswalala kadrowa so wasawliyana nakowa.
ch: B:不要，上次借的錢你都還沒還我。
en: B: No, you haven't paid me back the money you borrowed last time.

ab: kalaleketa ko kisalro ina ki’ingi so?
en: A: Can I borrow your clothes?

ab: eene, mwa malra.
en: B: Well, you take it yourself.

ab: makaleketa ko kisalro ina ‘acase so?
en: A: Can I borrow your book?

ab: wa ‘akisawlro ko ki Dreresai.
en: B: I lent it to Ciyaw. *[Ciyaw] Please change the names of the women of each tribe.

ab: ‘akisawlroai li kwani ‘alilidingi so, eene?
ch: A:請借給我你的腳踏車好嗎?
en: A: Can I borrow your bicycle, please?

ab: eene, ai sawlri so nakowa loza.
ch: B: 好，但是明天要還給我。
en: B: Okay, but you have to give it back to me tomorrow.

ab: odaodalenga, nako wa’iloko sa soko, ‘akisawlroai li sa soko, eene?
ch: A:下雨了，我沒有帶傘，借我傘好嗎?
en: A: It's raining, I don't have an umbrella, can you lend me your umbrella?

ab: eea kai soko li, ta tadrosa si soko!
ch: B: 我只有一把傘，我們一起撐吧!
en: B: I only have an umbrella, let's stick together!

ab: kaivai ka akisalro so nakowa ’aswlala ka ‘acase, naw swali mosowa,
ch: A:這是你上次借我的書，還給你。
en: A: This is the book you borrowed from me last time, I give it back to you.

ab: yakaiyana sa manemanenga ai ccele so ka ‘acase kawrivaili.
ch: B:還想看什麼書都可以跟我說。
en: B:You can tell me what else you want to read.

ab: ai sawlriya ko mosowa lroiga kai ‘aiso?
en: A: When do I have to pay you back?

ab: aikatoma, lo ikainga kai ‘aiso so sawlriyanga nakowa
ch: B:沒關係，你有錢的時候再說。
en: B: It's okay, you can talk about it when you have money.

ab: lroiga aisawlri so nakowa sa ‘aiso?
en: B: When are you going to pay me back?

ab: naw sawlringa moswa loza.
en: A: I'll return it to you tomorrow.

ab: ai sawlri so nakowa lroiga idra ‘acase?
en: B: When are you going to return my book?

ab: kainako wasawlriyanga moswa sa koza?
ch: A:我昨天不是已經還你了嗎?
en: A: Didn't I return it to you yesterday?

ab: kaivai ‘inpici akisalro moswa ko Te’a?
en: A: Did Kacaw lend you this pen? *[Kacaw] Please change the name to an ethnic male.

ab: eene, kisalro li iniya kaivaiyana.
en: B: Yes, I borrowed it from him today.

ab: kaivai ‘inpici akisalro mosowa ko Te’a?
en: A: Did you borrow this pen from Kacaw? *[Kacaw] Please change the name to an ethnic male.

ab: ini, kaivai langai ki tinali.
en: B: No, this is what mom bought.

ab: kadrowanga ka timo, mwa kisalro ki Sele’e sa bekela.
en: A: We're out of salt, let's go borrow some from Panay. *[Panay] Please change the name to an ethnic woman.

ab: eene, namwa kisalro
en: B: Okay, I'll borrow it.

ab: sawlriya miyanga ki Lege’a idra ‘aisoli.
en: B: Go tell Panay to give me back my money. *[Panay] Please change the name to an ethnic woman.

ab: thainonga ka kiyame mosowa sa ’aiso?
en: A: How much does she owe you?
